response:
the error occurs because your project is targeting .NET 7 which does not include WPF assemblies by default on all platforms and the runtime cannot locate the legacy presentation framework assembly.

you must configure your project to use conditional compilation or separate project structures so that WPF code is only included when building for windows and excluded for mac builds.

cousin solutions involve using the net7.0-windows target framework for windows specific code while keeping the main logic in a shared net7.0 class library that avoids direct WPF dependencies.

ensure that your windows specific project file explicitly includes the Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App package or targets net7.0-windows to resolve the missing assembly reference correctly.

this approach allows your application to remain cross platform by isolating the windows only UI components while maintaining a clean build process for mac os without triggering the file not found exception.
